Notes: Initial goes to Inactive rather than a scheduled waiting state but it represents preactivation.
Inactive partially represents the scheduled powered off train before activation.
scheduledStopBegins activates the train at the beginning of the initial stop period.
Activation performs pOn and sets the first segment with setCS.
Active composite contains the operating train behavior and monitoring regions.
Operation region represents the main train control behavior.
Journey composite represents the movement and stopping control.
Journey initially enters InitialStationStop which is the first station dwell.
Station dwell is represented by InitialStationStop and DwellingAtStation and final dwell.
Doors are opened on entering station dwell states and on initial activation.
Doors are closed when leaving station dwell states.
Initial and intermediate station dwell transition to Running after the stop duration.
Nonfinal behavior is encoded by using separate nonfinal station stopping logic.
Final station dwell powers off after the stop duration by transitioning to Inactive.
Final station behavior is selected with the finalStation guard before the final dwell.
FinalStationDwelling performs pOff after the stop duration.
Running represents normal travel when no stop is currently required.
Speed is set on transitions into and within Running when travel should continue.
Running self transition handles entry to a regular segment.
Regular segment condition is explicitly checked.
Traffic light red segment leads to StoppingAtRedLight.
Guard checks traffic light segment and red light.
Traffic light segment with nonred light continues Running.
Guard checks traffic light segment and not red.
Required nonfinal station stop leads to StoppingAtStation.
Station stop is guarded by station segment and station on itinerary.
StoppingAtRedLight represents approaching a required red light stop.
stopAL is called when transitioning to StoppingAtRedLight.
Stopped detection moves to WaitingAtRedLight.
WaitingAtRedLight represents waiting at a red signal.
Green light transition resumes Running with setSpeed.
StoppingAtStation represents approaching a station stop.
stopAL is called when a station stop is required.
Stopped detection moves from station approach to station dwell and opens doors.
H and obstructionCleared to H capture resuming the interrupted journey.
EmergencyStopped represents the emergency stop condition.
ObstructionDetected moves Journey to EmergencyStopped.
Emergency transition performs eStop.
ObstructionCleared returns from EmergencyStopped to history.
SegmentDetection region models the segment detection system.
SegmentDetection initially enters MonitoringSegment.
MonitoringSegment is the active SDS monitoring state.
MonitoringSegment checks for a new segment every second.
enteredNewSegment is the equivalent segment change guard.
On new segment it sets current segment and raises segmentUpdated.
MonitoringSegment also self loops every second when unchanged.
The no new segment guard is explicitly modeled.
MovementDetection region models the movement detection system.
MovementDetection initially enters Stopped.
Stopped state is present.
Stopped transitions to Moving when trainMoving is true.
trainMoving is a reasonable equivalent for positive speed.
Stopped self loop occurs when not moving.
!trainMoving is a reasonable equivalent for zero speed.
The Stopped self loop does not raise stoppedDetected.
Moving state is present.
Moving transitions to Stopped when train is no longer moving.
!trainMoving is a reasonable equivalent for zero speed.
Moving to Stopped raises stoppedDetected.
Moving self loop occurs while trainMoving is true.
trainMoving is a reasonable equivalent for positive speed.
ObstructionDetection region models the obstruction detection system.
ObstructionDetection initially enters TracksClear as the monitoring state.
TracksClear and TracksObstructed together represent active obstruction monitoring.
TracksClear detects obstruction after one second and triggers the event.
tracksObstructed is the equivalent obstruction guard.
raiseObstructionDetected initiates the emergency transition.
TracksClear self loops every second when no obstruction is present.
!tracksObstructed is the equivalent no obstruction guard.
No distinct Arrived state is modeled but final power off goes to Inactive.
Extra states such as separate final dwell and obstruction states are reasonable refinements.
Extra transitions for final station handling and obstruction clearing are consistent with the description.
Extra guards such as finalStation and stationOnItinerary refine required behavior.
Extra event raising and door actions support the modeled behavior.
No fundamentally incorrect extra composite states are present.
No fundamentally incorrect extra regions are present.
The extra named history element is intended for emergency resume behavior.
